falsification_conditions.condition: The price closes above the previous 52-week high by at least 10% within 14 days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: A significant upward breakout would contradict the pattern's implied bearish structure, invalidating the 'UNKNOWN' verdict by proving a bullish reversal

falsification_conditions.condition: Volume surges to 150% of the 30-day average daily volume on a single day with a price increase of 3% or more
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: This would indicate strong buying pressure and market conviction in a directional move, directly refuting the 'HOLD' signal and blowoff score's implied indecision

falsification_conditions.condition: The S&P 500 closes above its 200-day moving average by 2% or more within 14 days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: Breaking above this key trendline would invalidate the pattern's bearish implications and prove the current call wrong by demonstrating a confirmed bullish trend

doctrine: If you can't list what would prove your call wrong, you don't have a real call.
